headline: Cash in circulation swung from a 20.2% contraction to a 37.7% surge within one year after demonetisation, and cash intensity almost returned to pre-shock levels by 2019-20.
story: RBI data show that currency in circulation growth fell to -20.2% in 2016-17, then jumped to +37.7% in 2017-18. Over the same period, the currency-GDP ratio dropped from 12.1% (2015-16) to 8.7% (2016-17), then climbed back to 12.0% by 2019-20. The pattern is a sharp collapse followed by an unusually fast re-cashification.
surprise: The policy shock was designed to compress cash dependence, but the medium-term data show a near-complete rebound in cash intensity.
